tool.name: featmerge
tool.summary: Merge selected right-hand features into identifier-matched annotated records

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `featmerge` merges selected features from a right-hand annotated input into identifier-matched left-hand annotated records using the shared Epithema feature-copy and annotated-record seams. The left-hand residues and metadata are preserved, and exact duplicate features are skipped deterministically.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 tool accepts two annotated EMBL or GenBank inputs plus optional feature selectors. Record pairing is by exact identifier set, paired lengths must match, and selectors apply only to right-hand features before the merge.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: One output FASTA record is emitted per left-hand record in stable input order. Left-hand features are preserved first, admitted right-hand features are appended in stable source order, and exact duplicate features are not re-added.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema featmerge`. Rust tests currently cover successful merges, deterministic duplicate skipping, no-merge failure when a selector admits only duplicates, and service invocation against committed annotated fixtures.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 merge is structural rather than source-textual: it preserves the normalized Epithema feature model, not original flatfile formatting. Record pairing is strict, duplicate identifiers are rejected, and more elaborate overlap reconciliation is intentionally deferred.

artifacts.label: Left-hand annotated GenBank fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed left-hand annotated fixture used for feature-merge validation.

artifacts.label: Right-hand annotated GenBank fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed right-hand annotated fixture containing one duplicate feature and one mergeable feature.

examples.title: Merge selected right-hand annotations into left-hand records
examples.description: Merges the right-hand annotated fixture into the left-hand record set and admits only the non-duplicate right-hand feature.

expected_outputs.label: Feature-merged sequences
expected_outputs.description: The left-hand record is preserved and one non-duplicate feature is appended from the right-hand record.
